commands.review_summary:
The PR changes iMessage approval result updates so normalized `sms:` handle targets omit `replyToId` while chat targets and non-SMS iMessage handles keep threaded updates.

PR surface: Source +12. Total +12 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection at medium confidence: current main prepares normalized `sms:` targets and `updateEntry` unconditionally passes `replyToId`. I did not run a live iMessage/SMS repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has a protected maintainer label and the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof of live iMessage/SMS behavior, not a concrete automated code repair.

target.title: fix(imessage): avoid threaded sms approval updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Prevents iMessage SMS approval result updates from being sent as threaded replies to the original approval prompt.
- Keeps normal iMessage and chat-target approval updates threaded, preserving existing behavior outside SMS handles.
- Isolates the SMS fix to the iMessage approval handler; broader native approval route-gate refactors and inbound delivery targeting changes are intentionally out of scope.
- Review should focus on whether SMS handle targets should omit `replyToId` while all other iMessage targets continue to include it.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #87770

Requested by maintainer in the PR isolation workflow.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: SMS approval updates should preserve SMS delivery behavior instead of sending result messages as threaded iMessage replies.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS; branch created from current `origin/main` and pushed to `origin/codex/imessage-sms-approval-fix`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`; `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Branch diff is limited to `extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.
- Observed result after fix: SMS handle targets no longer pass `replyToId` when sending approval update messages; non-SMS iMessage targets still pass `replyToId`.
- What was not tested: Live iMessage/SMS approval flow was not rerun on this isolated branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This PR intentionally isolates the code change and relies on CI plus focused diff proof; no live phone-number proof is included in the PR body.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, `updateEntry` always passed `replyToId: entry.messageId` for all iMessage approval targets, including `sms:` handles.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD`

Regression coverage added or updated: None; this is a narrow runtime routing change isolated from the broader native approval helper refactor.

What failed before this fix, if known: SMS approval update delivery was mixed with threaded iMessage reply behavior because `replyToId` was passed unconditionally.

If no test was added, why not? The change is isolated to one target-preparation branch and is being split out from the broader PR for review; CI will run the standard gates.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. SMS approval result messages should no longer be sent as threaded replies.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The iMessage approval update path for SMS handle targets.

How is that risk mitigated?

The condition only suppresses `replyToId` for parsed `sms:` handle targets; parse failures and non-SMS targets retain the previous threaded behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run CI and review the isolated iMessage approval-handler diff.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and any maintainer-requested live iMessage proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet on this isolated PR.
target.author: kevinslin
